title: Citrus decumana
taxonomy: Citrus decumana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family consists of approximately 160 genera and over 1,000 species, including various types of citrus fruits such as oranges, lemons, and limes. Citrus decumana is part of the genus Citrus, which comprises around 16 species of flowering plants. The genus Citrus is further divided into two subgenera: Citrus and Pomelia, with Citrus decumana belonging to the subgenus Citrus. The classification of Citrus decumana provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Citrus decumana, also known as the Buddha's hand, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The peel of the Buddha's hand is also used to make a tea that is said to help with weight loss and improve circulation. Additionally, the essential oil extracted from the peel is used in aromatherapy to reduce stress and promote relaxation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Citrus decumana.
